title: Moosend vs Constant Contact (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Moosend vs Constant Contact. See pricing, features, and ratings to find the best email marketing tool for your business.
h1: Moosend vs Constant Contact: Complete Comparison
intro: Moosend and Constant Contact are both established email marketing platforms, but they serve different business needs. Moosend emphasizes affordability and automation for growing businesses, while Constant Contact focuses on simplicity and integrated tools for nonprofits and local enterprises. Understanding their key differences will help you choose the platform that best fits your marketing goals and budget.
choose_a_if: You want a free tier with unlimited email sends to get started
You need advanced automation workflows and landing pages without breaking the budget
You prioritize real-time analytics and A/B testing capabilities
choose_b_if: You need built-in e-commerce and SMS marketing features
You want to manage social media ads and events from one platform
You're a nonprofit or local business looking for integrated tools

faq.q: Is Moosend better than Constant Contact?
faq.a: Moosend edges out Constant Contact overall due to superior customer ratings (4.7 vs 4.0 on G2) and a more generous free tier with unlimited emails. However, Constant Contact offers more integrated features like e-commerce and SMS, making it better for certain use cases. The best choice depends on your specific needs and budget.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Moosend or Constant Contact?
faq.a: Moosend is significantly cheaper, offering a free plan with up to 1,000 subscribers and unlimited emails, then starting at just $9/month for paid plans. Constant Contact has no free tier and begins at $12/month. For budget-conscious businesses, Moosend is the clear winner.

faq.q: Can I switch from Constant Contact to Moosend?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms typically allow you to export your contact lists and email templates. Most email marketing tools support standard CSV and import formats, though you may need to recreate some automation workflows manually. Check each platform's documentation for specific migration guidelines.
